rationale: Keep this pair because the citing paper is centrally about asymmetric dominance/attraction and context effects, while the cited paper explicitly addresses context-effect modeling and includes asymmetric dominance/decoy-related effects in its scope. That makes the 2004 paper a plausible theory/foundational source rather than an obviously incidental mention. The DOI-level match confirms a real citation, and there is no evidence here that it is merely a perfunctory background list item.
